frameworks.name: cortical feedback processing
frameworks.one_line: top-down signals to V1 carry non-physical sensory content

frameworks.name: laminar cortical microcircuit
frameworks.one_line: deep vs superficial V1 layers index distinct feedback streams

core_question: Do mental imagery and visual illusions recruit dissociable laminar microcircuits in primary visual cortex?
key_assumptions: feedback content is readable from V1 activation patterns
laminar fMRI can separate deep- and superficial-layer signals
imagery and illusion are both non-physical visual experiences
manipulations: type of internal experience (imagery vs illusion vs perception)
cortical depth (deep vs middle vs superficial V1 layers)
key_findings: imagery content decodable mainly from deep V1 layers
illusory content decodable mainly from superficial V1 layers
illusory and perceptual content share decodable information
imagery does not generalise to illusory or perceptual codes
interpretations: illusions and imagery rely on partially distinct early visual microcircuits
overlapping recruitment may still emerge from nuances of subjective experience
